aliases: the sleep cycle of agent memory
attach synthesis cluster to topic graph
blurb: The offline sleep cycle: how working rows are deduped, dreamed over, decayed, synthesized, and retired.
intent: Explain `reflect()` — the batch consolidation cycle that turns raw `working` rows into curated `episodic` knowledge — as four ordered stages. (1) A mechanical no-model pre-pass: near-duplicate dedup (`_dedup_merge`, embedding cosine or `difflib` text-ratio fallback), the pre-decay engagement sweep (`_score_pending`), and legacy `kind='digest'` retirement. (2) The single agentic `dream` LLM stage (`_dream`): one `llm.complete` over a bounded evidence pack (`_build_dream_pack`) of pending working rows with their co-retrieval neighbours plus up to `contradiction_budget` suspect same-referent episodic pairs, returning one JSON plan (promote/hold/drop/merge, contradict/obsolete/distinct, synthesize) applied deterministically with per-action validation (`_apply_dream_plan`), blind-promote on model outage. (3) Lifecycle decay: forget-stale (`_forget_stale`), chronically-ignored importance decay reading the soft/hard engagement split (`_decay_chronically_ignored`/`_decay_reason`), and stale-reference flagging with git rename-follow (`_flag_stale_references`). (4) Embed (`_embed_episodic`) + `related`-edge harvest (`_harvest_edges`). `topic_attach.py` lands synthesis clusters on the human-approved `.regin/topics` graph.
label: Memory consolidation (reflect: mechanical pre-pass, agentic dream, lifecycle decay, embed & edges)
